journal-title-group.journal-title: Journal of Feline Medicine and Surgery

title-group.article-title: FelineVMA position statement on the socialization of feral kittens

permissions.copyright-holder: International Society of Feline Medicine and American Association of Feline Practitioners, unless otherwise noted. Manuscript content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Licenses

license.license-p: This article is distributed under the terms of the Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 License (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/4.0/) which permits non-commercial use, reproduction and distribution of the work without further permission provided the original work is attributed as specified on the SAGE and Open Access pages (https://us.sagepub.com/en-us/nam/open-access-at-sage).

body.p: The FelineVMA supports the proper socialization of kittens. A kitten is defined as any cat between birth and 1 year of age. 1 Socializing kittens before a certain age helps them develop appropriate social behaviors, including recognizing humans as a friendly species. This offers the best scenario for the formation of healthy cat-human bonds.

The FelineVMA supports the integration of newly homed companion kittens through appropriate socialization and habituation. 2 Companion kittens are those kittens who have been socialized with humans prior to being brought into their new home. 3 The sensitive socialization period for kittens occurs roughly between 2 and 9 weeks of age. A lack of positive interactions with humans during this sensitive socialization period leads cats to view humans as a non-friendly (threatening) species and can lead to a lifelong fear of humans.

A feral cat or kitten is often born outside, has been unsocialized to humans and would likely experience distress by living inside with humans. 3 Generally, the FelineVMA does not support the socialization of feral kittens over 4 months of age as it may be detrimental to their emotional health. 4 Because feral kittens are often born outside, kittens over the age of 4 months should undergo trap-neuter-return (TNR) or trap-neuter-vaccinate-return (TNVR) 5 rather than attempts at socialization. 4 Exceptions to using TNR may include cats and kittens born inside, but who are unsocialized, often due to situations such as cat hoarding, and where TNR would not be appropriate. The FelineVMA strongly encourages further research on various aspects of feline socialization.

ref.mixed-citation: QuimbyJGowlandSCarneyHCet al. 2021 AAHA/AAFP feline life stage guidelines. J Feline Med Surg2021 ; 23: 211-233.3362700310.1177/1098612X21993657PMC10812130
International Cat Care. Life stage guide to caring for your new kitten. www.icatcare.org/resources/icatcare-kitten-booklet-2024.pdf (2024, accessed 28 February 2025).

International Cat Care. Cat friendly decision-making: managing cat populations based on an understanding of cat lifestyle and population dynamics, jsmcah.org/index.php/jasv/article/view/58/46 (2022, accessed 6 March 2025).
Alley Cat Allies. The cat socialization continuum: a guide to interactions between cats and humans. www.alleycat.org/resources/cat-socialization-continuum-guide/ (2020, accessed 20 March 2024).
International Cat Care. Trap, neuter, return. www.icatcare.org/unowned-cats/feral-street-cats/trap-neuter-return/ (2022, accessed 24 July 2024).

sec.title: Additional resources
sec.p: Feral and free-ranging cat behavior literature
✜ The welfare of feral cats
Slater MR. In: Rochlitz I (ed). The welfare of cats. Dordrecht: Springer, 2007, pp 141-175.
✜ The social lives of free-ranging cats
Vitale KR Animals 2022; 12. DOI: 10.3390/ani12010126.
✜ Outdoor domestic cats and wildlife: how to overrate and misinterpret field data
Turner DC. Front Vet Sci 2022; 9. DOI: 10.3389/fvets.2022.1087907.
Human-cat relationship literature
✜ The effects of additional socialisation for kittens in a rescue centre on their behaviour and suitability as a pet
Casey RA and Bradshaw JWS. Appl Anim Behav Sci 2008; 114: 196-205.
✜ Coexistence between humans and ‘misunderstood’ domestic cats in the anthropocene: exploring behavioural plasticity as a gatekeeper of evolution
Natoli E, Litchfield C and Pontier D. Animals 2022; 12. DOI: 10.3390/ani12131717.
✜ A review of over three decades of research on cat-human and human-cat interactions and relationships
Turner DC. Behav Processes 2017; 141: 297-304.
✜ Unanswered questions and hypotheses about domestic
✜ Special issue on behavioural ecology and welfare of free-ranging cats
Litchfield C and Natoli Ε (eds). Animals 2022; 12. www.mdpi.com/journal/animals/special_issues/free_ranging_cats (accessed 28 February 2025).
✜ The feral guide
Cats Protection, www.cats.org.uk/media/10267/feral-guide-cats-protection.pdf (2021, accessed 28 February 2025).
cat behavior, ecology, and the cat-human relationship
Turner DC. Animals 2021 ; 11. DOI: 10.3390/ani11102823.
✜ The mechanics of social interactions between cats and their owners
Turner DC. Front Vet Sc/2021; 8. DOI: 10.3389/fvets.2021. 650143.
✜ What's inside your cat's head? A review of cat (Felis silvastris catus) cognition research past, present and future
Vitale Shreve KR and Udell MAR. Anim Cogn 2015; 18: 1195-1206.
✜ Methods of assessment of the welfare of shelter cats: a review
Vojtkovská V, Voslářová Ε and Večerek V. Animals 2020; 10. DOI: 10.3390/ani10091527.
